nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: Monte Desert

nodes.name: Straneck's tyrannulet
nodes.summary: Straneck's tyrannulet（Straneck's tyrannulet，学名 Serpophaga griseicapilla），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联13个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括稀树草原。
locations: The Clements Checklist of Birds of the World

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Straneck's tyrannulet (Serpophaga griseicapilla), also known as the monte tyrannulet and grey-crowned tyrannulet, is a small species of bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Paraguay, and Uruguay.Remsen, J

links.label: 分布于

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Straneck's tyrannulet (Serpophaga griseicapilla), also known as the monte tyrannulet and grey-crowned tyrannulet, is a small species of bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Paraguay, and Uruguay.Remsen, J

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: According to the South American Classification Committee of the American Ornithological Society, it breeds in Argentina and Bolivia and is a non-breeding visitor to Brazil, Paraguay, and Uruguay. BirdLife International's (BLI) map, used by the IUCN, shows it as breeding only in Argentina and as a non-breeder in northern Argentina, southern Bolivia, far southern Brazil, Paraguay, and Uruguay. The International Ornithological Committee generally agrees with BLI but questions Brazil. The Clements taxonomy does not include Bolivia in its range statement; it lists but does not specify breeding or non-breeding in the other four countries.Clements, J

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Downloaded from https://www.birds.cornell.edu/clementschecklist/download/ retrieved October 23, 2024 The Cornell Lab of Ornithology's Birds of the World describes it as breeding in northwestern Argentina and possibly southern Bolivia and as a non-breeder in eastern Bolivia, southern Brazil, Paraguay, and Uruguay. During the breeding season Straneck's tyrannulet inhabits the Monte Desert in Argentina

links.evidence: In its non-breeding range it inhabits the Chaco, the Argentine Espinal, Pampas grasslands, and lowland moist forest
